written.scope: adult mouse dentate gyrus, hilar mossy cells
written.quote_text: Glutamatergic hilar mossy cells (MCs) have axons that terminate both near and far from their cell body but stay within the DG, making synapses primarily in the molecular layer

written.scope: adult mouse dentate gyrus, hilar mossy cells
written.quote_text: Hilar mossy cells are the prominent glutamatergic cell type in the dentate hilus of the dentate gyrus (DG)

written.scope: rodent dentate gyrus, hilar mossy cells
written.quote_text: there are two glutamatergic principal cells instead of one: granule cells, which are the vast majority of the cells in the DG, and the so-called "mossy cells."

written.scope: rodent dentate gyrus, hilar mossy cells
written.quote_text: mossy cells (MCs), a major DG cell type that is glutamatergic and innervates the primary output cells of the DG, the granule cells (GCs)

written.scope: rodent dentate gyrus, hilar mossy cells (dorsal and ventral)
written.quote_text: The hippocampus has been studied for many decades for its largely known roles in encoding spatial memory, and a growing body of evidence indicates a differential involvement of dorsal and ventral hippocampal divisions in novelty detection
